name: E2E: a rule scraped from a source (no-privileged-containers) routes by kind to checkov/kubescape AND the materialized rule blocks a privileged pod — source to enforcement
command:
printf '# K8s Security

## No privileged containers
Kubernetes pods must not run privileged containers.
' > k.md && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/extract-rules-from-url.sh" --source k.md --source-id k8s-sec --json > rules.json 2>/dev/null && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/classify-rule.sh" --title 'No privileged' --prose 'Kubernetes pods must not run privileged containers.' --json > c.json 2>/dev/null && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/route-rule.sh" --in c.json --json > r.json 2>/dev/null && node -e 'const c=require(process.cwd()+"/c.json"),r=require(process.cwd()+"/r.json");if(!c.applies_to.iac_dialects.includes("kubernetes"))process.exit(1);if(!r.enforced_by.some(x=>x.tool==="checkov"||x.tool==="kubescape"))process.exit(1)' && printf 'apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
spec:
  containers:
  - securityContext:
      privileged: true
' > pod.yaml && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/enforce-file.sh" --file pod.yaml 2>ef.txt; ec=$?; [ "$ec" -eq 1 ] && grep -q 'g-k8s-no-privileged-container' ef.txt && echo e2e4-source-to-enforcement 1>&2
